messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is High Availability?

High Availability: Key goals of HA include:
- Achieve five-nines (99.999%) availability of switching traffic through minimization of unplanned network outages.
- Fault tolerant: No single active component failure will cause an outage.
- Live replacement of hardware with minimal or no disruption.
Terminology:
- MM: Abbreviation for management module - MM to MM link: Refers to the 10GbE-KR Ethernet link between two MMs - OVSDB: Abbreviation for Open vSwitch Database - Active MM: Management module that has control of the chassis - Standby MM: Backup management module for the active management module - JSON-RPC: Remote procedure call protocol encoded in JSON
Key parts of the HA feature include: Network redundancy: Protocols and redundant network paths provide redundancy in the network, enabling traffic to continue flowing if a network link or network switch fails. Hardware redundancy: Redundant hardware components (power supplies, fabric cards, management modules) allow continued switching traffic or system management in the event of a hardware failure or hardware maintenance.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is Management Module Failover?

Management Module Failover: There are two types of Management Module (MM) failover:
- Controlled failover: The user triggers this type of failover by rebooting the Active MM or running the redundancy switchover command.
- Uncontrolled failover: This type of failover is triggered by unexpected events like a crash on the Active MM or hot removal of the Active MM. In a dual MM chassis, the Standby MM detects failover events in one of the following ways:
- A mailbox interrupt is received from the Active MM to indicate takeover. This interrupt can come for controlled or uncontrolled failover (except for a hot removal).
- Active MM hot removal detection.
- Heartbeat loss detected on the Standby MM for more than 10 seconds. If the Active MM is not responding and is still not detected by the first two methods, it will be caught by this method.
Failover requirements:
- The Standby MM must be present to trigger a failover. An Unassigned MM will never trigger a failover.

section: AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules?

AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules: Consider the following when working with local authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) on switches with multiple management modules:
- Local authentication: - The user database is synchronized between the Active and Standby management modules. - Only local users belonging to the administrators group and using local password authentication are permitted to log in to the Standby management module. Alternatively, the Standby management module can be accessed from the Active management module by providing a logged in admin user password.
- Local authorization: - A few nonconfiguration commands are available on the Standby management module. - For expert users, the bash shell is available on the Standby management module.
- Local accounting: - The audit logs used for local accounting are available only on the Active Management Module.

topic: AAA on Switches with Multiple Management Modules

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the redundancy switchover command do?

The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present. Syntax: redundancy switchover.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the redundancy switchover command?
Syntax: redundancy switchover
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is BFD?

BFD: The BFD feature and thus this entire chapter is not applicable to the 6200 Switch Series. Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) provides a general-purpose, standard, medium- and protocol- independent fast failure detection mechanism. It can detect and monitor the connectivity of links in IP to detect communication failures quickly. BFD operates independently of media, data protocols, and routing protocols. BFD establishes a session between two network devices to detect failures on the bidirectional forwarding paths between the devices and provide services for upper-layer protocols. BFD provides no neighbor discovery mechanism. Protocols that BFD services notify BFD of devices to which it needs to establish sessions. After a session is established, if no BFD control packet is received from the peer within the negotiated BFD interval, BFD notifies a failure to the protocol, which then takes appropriate measures. BFD operates in two modes:
- Asynchronous mode: In this mode, an operating device periodically sends BFD control packets to another device.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about BFD Features?

BFD Features: BGP, OSPFv2, OSPFv3, PIMv4 and PIMv6, static routes, and VRRP are clients of BFD.
Supported:
- BFD v1 - Asynchronous mode + echo - IPv6 (8400, 6400 and 6300 switches only) - Asynchronous mode on IPv6 tunnel interfaces (8400 switches only) - Asynchronous mode for VxLAN tunnels (8325, 8360 and 8400 switches only) - Single hop - IPv4 - RoP, SVI, and LAG interfaces - VSX synchronization. For more information, see the Virtual Switching Extension (VSX) Guide for your switch and software version.
- Loopbacks are supported for VxLAN sessions (8325, 8360 and 8400 switches only), and static routes (6300, 6400 and 8400 switches only). Same IP version restrictions apply.
Not supported:
- MIB support - IPv6 (832x switches only) - Demand mode - Micro-BFD - Authentication - Echo function on tunnel interfaces - BFD sessions are not supported on tunnel interfaces (6300, 6400, and 8320 switches only) - IPv6 BFD sessions are not supported (8320, 8325, and 8360 switches only) - Echo function for IPv6 - Asynchronous mode on tunnel interfaces (832x switches only) - Multi-hop configurations.

section: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, how do you configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route?
To configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on an IPv4 static route with the command ip route bfd.

task: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd command do?

The bfd command enables BFD support on the switch. BFD is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables BFD and removes all related configuration settings. To disable BFD, but retain configuration settings, use the command bfd disable. Syntax: bfd.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd command?
Syntax: bfd
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command?
Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or PIM IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 ospfv3 bfd. Syntax: bfd all-interfaces.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
Syntax: bfd all-interfaces
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd detect-multiplier command do?

The bfd detect-multiplier command sets BFD detection multiplier on an interface. The no form of this command sets the BFD detection multiplier to the default value of 5. Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier | <MULTIPLIER>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command?
Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier | <MULTIPLIER>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd disable command do?
The bfd disable command disables BFD on the switch, but retains all configuration settings. Syntax: bfd disable.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
Syntax: bfd disable
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd enable command do?
The bfd enable command enables or disables BFD for HSC feature. Syntax: bfd enable.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd enable command?
Syntax: bfd enable
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd echo disable command do?

The bfd echo disable command disables support for BFD echo packets. Echo packet support is enabled by default. The no form of this command enables support for BFD echo packets. Toggling this feature on 8325 or 8360 switches may cause route flapping. Syntax: bfd echo disable.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd echo disable command?
Syntax: bfd echo disable
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command?
Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address | <IPv4-ADDR>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd min-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd min-transmit-interval command do?

The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between transmitted BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 ms. Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear bfd statistics command do?
The clear bfd statistics command clears statistics for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: clear bfd | statistics | [session | <ID>].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear bfd statistics command?
Syntax: clear bfd | statistics | [session | <ID>]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd command?
Syntax: ip ospf | bfd
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip ospf bfd disable command do?
The ip ospf bfd disable command disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command?
Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip route bfd command do?

The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip route bfd command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command disables BFD on the current OSPFv3 interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the neighbor fall-over bfd command?
Syntax: neighbor | {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} | | | | fall-over | | bfd
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show bfd command do?
The show bfd command showsinformationforall BFD sessionsorforaspecific BFD session. Syntax: show bfd | [session | <ID>] | [all-vrfs | | vrf | <NAME>] | [vsx-peer].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show bfd command?
Syntax: show bfd | [session | <ID>] | [all-vrfs | | vrf | <NAME>] | [vsx-peer]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show bfd interface command?
Syntax: show bfd interface | | <NAME>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show hsc command do?
The show hsc command displaysconnectioninformationfortheremotecontroller. Syntax: show hsc.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show hsc command?
Syntax: show hsc
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is ERPS?

ERPS: supported on the following switches:
- 6300 - 6400 - 8320 - 8325 - 8360 - 8400 Ethernet Ring Protection Switching (ERPS) is a protocol defined by the International Telecommunication Union - Telecommunication Standardization Sector (ITU-T) to eliminate loops at Layer 2. Because the standard number is ITU-T G.8032/Y1344, ERPS is also called G.8032. ERPS defines Ring Auto Protection Switching (RAPS) Protocol Data Units (PDUs) and protection switching mechanisms. ERPS has two versions:
- ERPSv1 released by ITU-T in June 2008, and - ERPSv2 released in August 2010. EPRSv2, fully compatible with ERPSv1, provides the following enhanced functions:
- Multi-ring topologies, such as intersecting rings - RAPS PDU transmission on non-virtual-channels (NVCs) in sub-rings - Forced Switch (FS) and Manual Switch (MS) - Revertive and non-revertive switching Generally, redundant links are used on an Ethernet switching network such as a ring network to provide link backup and enhance network reliability.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command do?

The clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode. Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command?
Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear erps statistics command do?
The clear erps statistics command this command clears the ERPS statistics for a ring or a ring instance. Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear erps statistics command?
Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring command do?
The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command do?

The erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> description command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance command on a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. When the ERPS ring is in normal state, the blocked port prohibits all service packets from passing through. As a result, all service data is transmitted through one path over the ERPS ring, and the other link on the blocked port becomes idle, leading to ineffective use of bandwidth. To improve link use efficiency, logical rings can be configured in the same physical ring in the ERPS multi- instance. A port may have different roles in different ERPS rings and different ERPS rings use different control VLANs. An ERPS ring must be configured with an ERP instance, and each ERP instance specifies a range of VLANs. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command?
Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command?
Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command?
Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command This command Specifies thesetof VLAN sthatareprotectedbythisringinstance. The no formofthiscommandremovesasetof VLAN sthatareprotectedbythisringinstance. Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command?
Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command do?

The erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command this command is to configure a sub-ring interconnection node to pass a topology change notification to the ring instance for the parent ring whenever the topology of the sub-ring changes. The parent ring instance performs a Forwarding Database (FDB) flush and sends a protocol message to ensure that other nodes on the parent ring also perform an FDB flush. The no form of this command disables topology change notifications. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command the RPL owner node uses a delay timer before initiating an RPL block in case of both revertive mode of operation or before reverting to idle state after clearing operator commands (FS, MS). The Wait to Restore (WTR) timer can be configured in 1-minute increments up to 12 minutes. The default value is 5 minutes. When recovering from an SF, the delay timer must be long enough to allow the recovering network to become stable. In the default revertive mode of operation, the WTR timer is used to prevent frequent operation of protection switching due to intermittent SF defects. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the wtr-interval and sets it to the default value of 5 minutes. Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps statistics command do?

The show erps statistics command this command displays ERPS statistics. The statistics can be displayed for the ring, the instance, or the instance ports. Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps statistics command?
Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps status command do?

The show erps status command this command displays detailed information about a specific ring or all instances of a ring. The ring instance may be in one of the following states:
- Idle: The ring instance is operational.
- Initializing: The ring instance is not operational.
- Protection: Protection switching has been triggered by a local or remote link failure.
- Pending: Pending clearance of a previous protection switch.
- Down: Ring instance is not active.
- Manual-switch: Manual protection switching triggered by Admin-down.
- Force-switch: Forced protection switching triggered by admin. A ring instance has the following reasons for "down" state:
- Disabled: Ring instance is administratively disabled. Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps status command?
Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps summary command do?
The show erps summary command thiscommanddisplaysasummaryof the ERPSconfigurationandstatefortheERPSringinstances. Syntax: show erps summary.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps summary command?
Syntax: show erps summary
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Configuration consistency requirements?

Configuration consistency requirements:
- Configure at least one active mode aggregation in two devices.
- For a successful static aggregation, make sure the ports at both ends of each link are in the same aggregation state.
- For a successful dynamic aggregation, make sure the peer ports of the ports aggregated at one end are also aggregated, and that one of the ends is configured as "active". The two ends can automatically negotiate the aggregation state of each member port. Removing interfaces - Deleting an aggregate interface also deletes its aggregation group and causes all member ports to leave the aggregation group.
- When a member interface is removed from a LAG: - 6100, 6200, 6300, and 6400 switches: The interface goes to its default status of unshut. - 8320, 8325, 8360, or 8400 switches: The interface becomes disabled. Disabling an interface When an interface LAG is disabled with the shutdown command, all its members also become operationally down. Layer 2 aggregation groups All switches support static and dynamic layer 2 aggregation groups.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the interface lag command do?
The interface lag command createsa Link Aggregation Group(LAG)interfacerepresentedbyanID. The no formofthiscommanddeletesa LAG interfacerepresentedbyanID. Syntax: interface lag | <ID>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the interface lag command?
Syntax: interface lag | <ID>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets an IPv4 address and subnet mask to a LAG interface. One primary and up to 31 secondary address can be configured per interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show lacp aggregates command do?
The show lacp aggregates command displaysallLACPaggregateinformationconfiguredforall LAGs, orforaspecificLAG. Syntax: show | lacp | aggregates | [<LAG-NAME>] | | | [vsx-peer].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show lacp aggregates command?
Syntax: show | lacp | aggregates | [<LAG-NAME>] | | | [vsx-peer]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show lacp interfaces command?
Syntax: show lacp | interfaces | [<IFNAME>] | [vsx-peer]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the vlan trunk native command?
Syntax: vlan trunk native | <VLAN-ID>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what restrictions or notes are documented for Guidelines and limitations?

For Guidelines and limitations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations:
- For faster convergence of routed traffic over Smartlink ports, ip neighbor-flood must be enabled on respective SVI interfaces.
- Smartlink uses ERPS copp class for flush packets. Limitations:
- VSX, ISL and MCLAGs cannot be included in Smartlink groups.
- Switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled exclude Smartlink ports from STP.
- On switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled, loops involving Smartlink and STP are not detected.
- On switches with both Smartlink and ERPS enabled, loops involving Smartlink and ERPS are not detected.
- ERPS and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Dynamic VLANs (MVRP) and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Loop Protect and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Multicast fast convergence is not supported.
- Uplink Failure Detection (UFD) is not supported.
- MIB and WebUI are not supported..
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show smartlink group command do?
The show smartlink group command shows information for a specific Smartlink group. Syntax: show smartlink group <GROUP-ID>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show smartlink group command?
Syntax: show smartlink group <GROUP-ID>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show running-config command do?
The show running-config command showscurrentrunningconfiguration. Syntax: show running-config.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show running-config command?
Syntax: show running-config

section: Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API?

Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API: The Aruba 6100 Switch Series only supports the default VRF and has no management port. Therefore, references in this guide to other VRFs or the management port do no apply to the 6100 Switch Series. Configuration for these switches should be done over an SVI having a physical port with access to the SVI, since the physical ports in the 6100 are not routed. Switches running the AOS-CX software are fully programmable with a REST (REpresentational State Transfer) API, allowing easy integration with other devices both on premises and in the cloud. This programmability- combined with the Aruba Network Analytics Engine-accelerates network administrator understanding of and response to network issues. The AOS-CX REST API is a web service that performs operations on switch resources using HTTPS POST, GET, PUT, and DELETE methods. The AOS-CX REST API enables programmatic access to the AOS-CX configuration and state database at the heart of the switch. By using a structured model, changes to the content and formatting of the CLI output do not affect the programs you write.

topic: Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API

messages.content: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Configuration attributes?

Configuration attributes: represent user-owned data. Although an attribute must be in the configuration category to be modified by a user, not all attributes in the configuration category can be modified after the resource instance is created. Configuration attributes that cannot be changed after the resource is created are called immutable attributes. This distinction matters when using a PUT request, because immutable attributes cannot be included in the request body. For example, a VLAN ID is an immutable attribute. You cannot change the ID of the VLAN after the VLAN is created. The VLAN name, in contrast, is a mutable (writable) attribute. You can change the VLAN name after the VLAN is created. Writable attributes Writable attributes are the subset of configuration attributes that are mutable. Writable attributes can be modified by a user after the resource is created. When using the PUT method to modify a resource, only writable attributes can be included in the request body.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the https-server rest access-mode command do?
The https-server rest access-mode command changesthe RESTAPIaccessmode. Thedefaultmodeisread-write. Syntax: https-server | rest | access-mode | {read-only | | read-write}.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command?
Syntax: https-server | rest | access-mode | {read-only | | read-write}
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the https-server session close all command do?

The https-server session close all command invalidatesandclosesall HTTPS sessions. Allexisting WebUIand REST sessionsareloggedoutandallreal- timenotificationfeature Web Socketconnectionsareclosed. Syntax: https-server | session | close | all.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the https-server session close all command?
Syntax: https-server | session | close | all
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the https-server vrf command do?

The https-server vrf command configures and starts the HTTPS server on the specified VRF. HTTPS server features include the REST API and the web user interfaces. The no form of the command stops any HTTPS servers running on the specified VRF and removes the HTTPS server configuration. Syntax: https-server | vrf | <VRF-NAME>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the https-server vrf command?
Syntax: https-server | vrf | <VRF-NAME>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show https-server command do?

The show https-server command shows the status and configuration of the HTTPS server. The REST API and web user interface are accessible only on VRFs that have the HTTPS server features configured. Syntax: show https-server [vsx-peer].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show https-server command?
Syntax: show https-server [vsx-peer]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about VLAN interfaces?

VLAN interfaces: - VLAN interfaces are of type vlan.
- You can use the DELETE method to delete a VLAN interface. Example of creating a VLAN interface: Method and URI: POST "/rest/v10.04/system/interfaces" Request body: { "name": "vlan2", "vlan_tag": "/rest/v10.04/system/vlans/2", "vrf": "/rest/v10.04/system/vrfs/default", "type": "vlan" } Write methods (POST, PUT) supported in read-only mode The following switch resources support write methods (POST, PUT, or both) even when the REST API access mode is set to read-only:
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Configuration maximums?

Configuration maximums:
- Maximum number of subscribers per switch: - Maximum number of topics in one subscription message: 2000 Enabling the notifications subsystem on a switch The AOS-CX real-time notifications subsystem relies on the REST API, so the REST API must be enabled on the switch and VRF from which you want to receive notifications. HTTPS server must be enabled on the specified VRF. The VRF you specify determines from which network the HTTPS server can be accessed. You can enable access on multiple VRFs, including user-defined VRFs. Establishing a secure Web Socket connection through a web browser Prerequisites - Access to the switch REST API must be enabled. The REST API access mode can be either read-only or read/write.
- The web browser you use must support the secure Web Socket Protocol. Procedure 1. Open a web browser page and log in to the switch Web UI or the REST API. The session cookie is managed by the browser and is shared among browser tabs. 2.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, how do you troubleshoot?

To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Ensure that you save the session cookie returned from the login request 2. Log out from one of the existing sessions 3. Repeat the command and send the correct session cookie or modify the script to send the correct. Commands shown in the procedure include: REST API | response | codes; https-server | | vrf mgmt.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Reference failure?

Reference failure: Cause You attempted to delete a resource that is referenced by other resources. Typically, this error occurs for resources that have no clear parent in the resource hierarchy, such as ports. For example, the Reference failure response is returned if you attempt a DELETE request on a port. Action Remove all references to the resource. After all references to a resource are removed, the resource is deleted automatically. HTTP 401 error "Authorization Required" Symptom A REST request returns response code 401 and the response body contains the following text string: Authorization Required This response means that no valid session was found for the session token passed to the API. Solution 1 Cause The user attempting the request is not logged into the REST API for one of the following reasons:
- The user has not yet logged in.
- The user logged in but the session has expired. Action Log in to the REST API.

For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show rmon alarm command do?
The show rmon alarm command displays the RMONalarmconfigurations. Syntax: show rmon | alarm | [index <INDEX>].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show rmon alarm command?
Syntax: show rmon | alarm | [index <INDEX>]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmp agent-port command do?
The show snmp agent-port command displays SNMP agent UDP portnumber. Syntax: show snmp agent-port.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmp agent-port command?
Syntax: show snmp agent-port
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmp community command do?
The show snmp community command displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Syntax: show snmp community.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmp community command?
Syntax: show snmp community
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmp system command do?
The show snmp system command displays SNMP description, location, andcontactinformation. Syntax: show snmp system.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmp system command?
Syntax: show snmp system
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmp trap command do?
The show snmp trap command displaysallconfigured SNMP traps/informsreceivers. Syntax: show snmp trap.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmp trap command?
Syntax: show snmp trap
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmp vrf command do?
The show snmp vrf command aOS-CX10.07SNMP/MIBGuide Displays the VRF onwhichthe SNMP agentserviceisrunning. Syntax: show snmp vrf.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmp vrf command?
Syntax: show snmp vrf
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmpv3 context command do?
The show snmpv3 context command displaysallconfigured SNMP contexts. Syntax: show snmpv3 context.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 context command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 context
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmpv3 engine-id command do?

The show snmpv3 engine-id command displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmpv3 security-level command do?
The show snmpv3 security-level command displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show snmpv3 users command do?
The show snmpv3 users command displaysallconfigured SNMP v3users. Formoredetailsontheuserenabledstatus, seesnmpv3security-level. Syntax: show snmpv3 users.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 users command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 users
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command?
Syntax: snmp-server | agent-port | | <PORT>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server community command?
Syntax: snmp-server | community | | <STRING>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list command do?

The access-list command associates an ACL with the SNMP community. If an ACL is not associated with the SNMP community, the default access is allowed for all the hosts. The no form of this command removes association of the ACL with the SNMP community. Syntax: access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list command?
Syntax: access-list <ACL-NAME>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command?
Syntax: snmp-server historical-counters-monitor
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the snmp-server host command do?

The snmp-server host command configures a trap/informs receiver to which the SNMP agent can send SNMP v1/v2c/v3 traps or v2c informs. A maximum of 30 SNMP traps/informs receivers can be configured. The no form of this command removes the specified trap/inform receiver. Syntax: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>].
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server host command?
Syntax: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>]
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the snmp-server system-contact command do?
The snmp-server system-contact command sets SNMP contactinformation. The no formofthiscommandremovesthe SNMP contactinformation. Syntax: snmp-server | system-contact | | <INFO>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command?
Syntax: snmp-server | system-contact | | <INFO>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the snmp-server system-location command do?
The snmp-server system-location command sets the SNMP locationinformation. The no formofthiscommandremovesthe SNMP locationinformation. Syntax: snmp-server | system-location | <INFO>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-location command?
Syntax: snmp-server | system-location | <INFO>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configuresa VRF onwhichthe SNMP agentlistensforincomingrequests. Bydefault, the SNMP agentdoes notlistenonanyVRF.6100onlysupportsdefaultVRF. The SNMP agentcanlistenonmultiple VRFs. AOS-CX10.07SNMP/MIBGuide The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Syntax: snmp-server | vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server vrf command?
Syntax: snmp-server | vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the snmp-server trap command do?

The snmp-server trap command enables the SNMP traps. The SNMP traps are enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the SNMP traps. Syntax: snmp-server trap {cpu-utilization | memory-utilization | rmon-events}.
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server trap command?
Syntax: snmp-server trap {cpu-utilization | memory-utilization | rmon-events}
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the snmp-server trap vsx command?
Syntax: snmp-server | trap vsx
